media$description.$t:
A bad game with an awful community is a recipe for disaster.

Video starts at 1:22:40.

In all seriousness, this is actually a pretty important video to me that I've been wanting to make for nearly 2 years now. Over the past several years, Rec Room has continued to make awful decisions for the game that only hurt it and its community.

For those unaware, Rec Room is a free-to-play VR social game with in-game tools that let you play and create ŌĆ£roomsŌĆØ for others to enjoy. The game is available on pretty much any platform you can think of: PC/Steam, Oculus/Meta Quest, Xbox, Mobile, Switch, and the list goes on.

I love the game a lot, but there's no doubt the game has taken a turn to UGC to compete with other games trying to bank on the irrelevant ŌĆ£MetaverseŌĆØ craze. Roblox, Fortnite, VRChat, Horizon Worlds, and probably more. Along with that, the devs have lost their entire vision for what the game originally wanted to be, the addition of creator compensation only poisoned the content seen in the game, the community is one of the worst I've seen in any game, Rec Room is incredibly unstable and unoptimized, and they even tried marketing the game to children. This game has lost so much of its soul over the years that dozens of veteran players have had it with Rec Room and have since quit playing the game.

Guess this game ain't for me anymore.

Partially inspired by Pheonix God's ŌĆ£5 Years : A Rec Room RetrospectiveŌĆØ video (https://youtu.be/cm-V32tSXP4?si=g5jGZCVIRCChKVdK)

ŌĆ£PartiallyŌĆØ because I've been wanting to make this before I first watched his video.

https://rec.net/image/12004962

CHAPTERS:
0:00 - Intro
1:37 - A Brief History
5:43 - Blinded by a Vision
13:16 - Greedier and Greedier
31:36 - Rec Room Unoriginals
43:25 - ItŌĆÖs Not Just Rec Room
58:16 - Moderation Hell
1:12:29 - Sponsors, Advertisements, and Children
1:22:40 - Evilness or Incompetence?
1:28:34 - November 23, 2019

CREDITS:

A lot of the models/assets used were remodeled and modified by me. Original assets are property of Rec Room Inc.

In this oculus quest 2 video, I play free five nights at Freddys in vr with multiplayer! it was so fun playing free fnaf vr in multiplayer in rec room- its just like five nights at Freddys help wanted vr on the oculus quest 2 in rec room but its a free five nights at Freddys vr game! We also check out a free vr five nights at Freddys security breach mapin rec room gameplay! This was so fun to play free five nights at Freddy's fnaf vr on the oculus quest 2 in rec room!

#fivenightsatfreddysvr #oculusquest2 #jmancurly

title.$t: DirecTV Music for 1 hour

media$title.$t: directv music

title.$t: directv music

media$description.$t:
Also known as "blackout music" or the "To be announced music". When I say straight from the source I literally actually mean straight from the source!

Now I did this in March but I didn't have the original high quality source for Diddley Squat and Memory Loss... until now. I contained the both of them and now I have the whole thing in a very enjoyable quality. 

I have the VideoHelper albums in a lossy format though but 320kbps isn't bad. Though YouTube does compress it to a lower format so...  but does it matter really? nah, but when having it locally it does.

Most of the tracks are made by Joseph Saba & Stewart Winter so make sure to give them a applause for this. Absolute wonderful tracks.

(some of these are available to listen on streaming platforms under a different name. comment if you want to know what they are.)

Tracklist:
0:00 - Byrd Brain
1:28 - Semisweet Symphony
2:41 - Diddley Squat
4:04 - Throb
5:04 - Demolition D
6:14 - Mellencamping
7:19 - Canyon Guitar
8:40 - Ramona Lisa
9:56 - Dan Halen
11:12 - Memory Loss
12:24 - Alertech
13:30 - Wall Of Guitars
15:12 - Morning Grin
16:19 - Jake & Amanda

then the cycle repeats. sometimes the last 3 tracks don't play and just continues with Byrd Brain. idk why that happens.

If you want to download these tracks I'll try and see if I can one day but not sure if the songs are still copyrighted though but it doesn't seem to be.
